title: Rome on-chain program — identity, lifecycle, current build
description: First-class record for a deployed Solana program (rome-evm or supporting program) tracked by the Rome registry. Captures identity, current authority, current build provenance, lifecycle role, and the chains hosted by this program. Append-only history lives in the sibling upgrades.json and authority.json files.

programId.description: On-chain Solana program ID (base58). Rome convention for new deploys: prefix `RomeP` for mainnet rome-evm programs, `RomeD` for devnet rome-evm programs — produced via `solana-keygen grind --starts-with` in /deploy-program. Convention is enforced at deploy time by skill, not by schema (legacy/imported programs may not match). CI may warn on violations but not block.

cluster.description: Solana cluster this program is deployed on. Rome only targets mainnet and devnet.

status.description: Lifecycle of the on-chain program. live = serving traffic; decommissioning = no new chains, existing chains migrating off; retired = no chains hosted, binary still on chain; closed = `solana program close` ran, rent reclaimed, programId permanently unusable. **Mainnet immutability rule**: transitions AWAY from `live` on mainnet programs require human-tagged commits; no automation may flip mainnet status.

role.description: Role in the cluster's program portfolio. Exactly one program may hold role=primary per cluster (enforced by CI invariant against programs/index.json). secondary = production but not the default for new chains; rehearsal = test/staging program. decommissioning/retired/closed mirror status; redundant-but-explicit so role-based queries don't have to join with status.

rolePromotedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this program was last promoted into its current role. null for programs created at their current role.

roleHistory.description: Append-only role-transition log. First entry records initial role at deploy time.

programDataAddress.description: ProgramData account address — derived PDA holding the program binary. Use with getSignaturesForAddress to enumerate upgrade history on-chain.

currentAuthority.description: Who holds upgrade authority RIGHT NOW. Append history to authority.json. Authority key material is NOT stored in the registry — only the pubkey + storage-location metadata.

pubkey.description: Authority pubkey. For multisig, this is the multisig PDA address; for single-sig kinds, it's the keypair pubkey.

kind.description: How the authority is held. cold-ledger = hardware wallet (Ledger/Trezor); hot-keypair = local file on operator machine; gsm-keypair = Google Secret Manager (or equivalent); squads-v4-multisig = on-chain Squads V4 multisig PDA; frozen = authority transferred to a non-signing key (no upgrades possible by this path); burned = authority set to System Program 11...11 (program is permanently immutable).

operator.description: Email or handle of the human accountable for this authority. For multisig, the primary operator/coordinator.

ledger.description: Required when kind=cold-ledger. derivationPath is the form skills pass to `solana program deploy --keypair <path>`.

project.description: GCP project id (or AWS account / Vault namespace).

secretId.description: Secret name/path. The actual key material is fetched at deploy time.

secretManager.description: Required when kind=gsm-keypair. References where the keypair is stored — never the key material itself.

pathHint.description: Filesystem path hint where the operator keeps the keypair (e.g. '~/rome/keypair').

localKeypair.description: Required when kind=hot-keypair. Hint only — nothing in the registry references the file content.

multisig.description: Required when kind=squads-v4-multisig. multisigPda is what gets passed as --upgrade-authority; members carry per-signer metadata for skill UX (which device to plug in, etc.).

rotationLog.description: Pointer to the append-only authority rotation log.

current.description: Current build provenance — what code is running on-chain RIGHT NOW. Replaced atomically on each upgrade.

version.description: Semver tag if tagged; null otherwise.

gitSha.description: Full 40-char git SHA at the source repo.

gitRepo.description: https URL of the source repo.

buildFeatureFlags.description: Cargo features active at build time (e.g. ['testnet', 'custom-heap']).

solanaVersion.description: Solana CLI version used by `cargo build-sbf` (e.g. '3.0.0').

programDataSha256.description: SHA-256 of the on-chain programData bytes. CI verifies against `solana program show`.

binarySize.description: Size of the deployed binary in bytes.

deployedAt.description: UTC time of the deploy tx.

deployedBy.description: Email/handle of the operator who initiated the deploy.

ciRunUrl.description: GitHub Actions run URL for the build, if applicable.

chainsHosted.description: Slug references (e.g. '121226-marcus') of chains currently registered on this program. CI invariant: each entry must have chains/<slug>/chain.json#romeEvmProgramId == this programId.

chainsDecommissioned.description: Chains that were once hosted on this program but have since retired.

decommissionedAt.description: Set when chains have all migrated off this program. **Mainnet immutability rule**: setting this on a mainnet program requires a human-tagged commit; no automation may write to it. Devnet writes may be agent-driven.

closedAt.description: Set when `solana program close` reclaims rent. Mutually implies role=closed and status=closed. **Mainnet immutability rule**: setting this on a mainnet program requires a human-tagged commit; no automation may write to it. The on-chain `solana program close` itself for mainnet must also be operator-typed, never agent-invoked.
